plan: Trim SPY to reduce concentration and meet mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to favor income and stability.
Buy XLF to diversify into a strong new sector.
Watch PANW for a potential buy, adding quality tech.
Maintain a cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation, targeting around 1.5% per month. Risk is reduced by cutting leverage and diversifying, but growth potential is lower. Expect smaller drawdowns in market corrections.

plan: Trim SPY to meet the concentration mandate and reduce gross leverage.
Sell SOXL as it is flagged and below stop.
Trim SPAL to maintain a small, compliant leveraged ETF exposure.
Add to CRWD, pressing into a strong uptrending name.
Buy PANW, a high-scoring quality tech setup for growth.
Watch MSFT for a potential buy, adding another quality tech name.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. Leverage is reduced, and concentration is managed, blending broad index with quality tech. Expect moderate drawdowns with balanced growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to meet the concentration mandate and manage gross leverage.
Sell SOXL as it is flagged and below stop, reallocating capital.
Add to SPAL, increasing leveraged ETF exposure within limits.
Increase CRWD exposure, pressing into a strong uptrending name.
Buy PANW, leveraging its high risk/reward setup.
Buy NVDU, a high-scoring leveraged tech play for aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It utilizes leveraged ETFs and concentrates in high-scoring setups. Be aware that this approach carries significantly higher drawdown risk, and leveraged ETFs experience daily decay and rebalancing. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and free capital for income.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged exposure.
Trim CRWD and SMH to reduce high-beta single name and sector exposure.
Increase QQQI significantly to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability and future opportunities.
Focus on low drawdown and reliable income over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, prioritizing steady distributions. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta names, rotating into income-focused ETFs. While aiming for stability, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market selloffs. This is for income, not a savings account.
